formations_blocks: open/direct early phase with little intricate buildup
adjustments: New Zealand drop off into a 4-4-2 mid block when Egypt's defenders have the ball
New Zealand look to get Singh on the ball in midfield and play direct balls into Wood or diagonals to McCowatt/Just
key_matchups: McCowatt vs Shobeir on the right channel/cross
Marmoush vs New Zealand marker inside the box
Wood vs New Zealand defence on the run in behind
Salah vs Wood in transition after the turnover
Ziko vs New Zealand defenders at the right edge of the box
phases.summary: The opening phase is fast and direct, with both sides threatening early in transition rather than through sustained possession. New Zealand control tempo at times by funnelling play through Singh and aiming early balls forward, while Egypt’s attempts to build through the lines are less clean and more reliant on wide delivery and individual moments from Salah or Marmoush.

formations_blocks: New Zealand long-ball/direct block with frequent balls downfield to Wood
Egypt counter-attacking shape, breaking quickly when New Zealand commit bodies forward
Egypt short-corner/set-piece routine on the right
adjustments: New Zealand increasingly commit players to second balls after long passes
Egypt exploit space behind New Zealand's direct approach on the counter
Egypt use a short corner before delivering into the box
key_matchups: Just vs the Egyptian back line on runs in behind
Wood vs the Egyptian defenders in aerial duels
Marmoush vs Crocombe, cutting in from the left
phases.summary: New Zealand’s phase is built around a direct long-ball game to Wood, with runners and second-ball support creating pressure and the opening goal from a simple set-piece mark-up error. Egypt respond by using New Zealand’s advanced, commit-heavy shape to counter into space, with Marmoush’s dribble and shot forcing a save and showing the trade-off in the match’s structure. The overall pattern is end-to-end but fragmented, with New Zealand uncomfortable when they cannot immediately recover the second ball.

key_matchups: Mohamed Salah vs New Zealand's defensive line
Chris Wood against Egypt's long-ball defending
phases.summary: New Zealand went into the break leading 1-0 after a first half in which their clear plan was to use Chris Wood as a focal point for long balls, with Just and McCowatt adding energy around him. Egypt looked sluggish in possession and poorly organised in attack, though Salah’s quality on the right remained a threat; overall, the half was open early before fading, with New Zealand’s direct approach proving more effective than Egypt’s slow buildup.

formations_blocks: New Zealand defensive third / low block
Egypt sustained attacking pressure with high press after turnovers
adjustments: Salah moved to a more central area in the second half
Egypt quickly regained possession after New Zealand turnovers to maintain pressure
key_matchups: Hany vs New Zealand right side
Ziko vs New Zealand box coverage
phases.summary: Egypt came out after the break on the front foot, pinning New Zealand deep with rapid counter-pressing and sustained possession around the box. A subtle but important adjustment saw Salah operate more centrally, helping Egypt combine more fluidly through the middle and create repeated openings, while New Zealand struggled to escape their own defensive third except for one dangerous chance of their own.

formations_blocks: Egypt defending deeper after going 3-1 up
New Zealand chasing the game with set-piece pressure and direct attacks
adjustments: Egypt substitutions: Marmoush and Ziko on for Trezeguet and Abdelkarim at 75'
Egypt later withdraw Salah and Ashour for Zizo and Hossam at 84' to protect the lead
New Zealand substitutions: Thomas and Randall on for Singh and Cacace at 75'
New Zealand later add Binden and De Vries for Just and Payne at 85' to increase attacking threat
key_matchups: Salah vs the New Zealand defense on the right
Ashour vs the New Zealand defense on the right
Ibrahim vs Wood on a set piece
Trezeguet vs New Zealand's marking at the near post on the corner
phases.summary: Egypt refused to sit on the 2-1 lead and kept attacking through Salah and Ashour, with their right-sided play repeatedly stretching New Zealand. After the third goal from a poorly defended corner, Egypt shifted to game management by removing their key attackers, while New Zealand turned increasingly direct with substitutions and throws but were repeatedly denied by Egypt’s defending.
